published_prior_art.cite: DoudchenkoImbens2016: allows signed/non-convex synthetic-control weights but does not state the bounded-error support-function interval

repo_prior_art.cite: pid_synth_control_factor_misspec angle0: row-span formula for oracle Chebyshev bridge intervals

published_prior_art.cite: CattaneoFengTitiunik2021: probabilistic prediction intervals; NazaretShiBlei2024: misspecification-error bounds, not this deterministic sharp fixed-weight interval
KelloggMogstadPouliotTorgovitsky2021: MASC trades interpolation/extrapolation bias but does not give equal-prefit sharp-radius separation
FirpoPossebom2018, ChernozhukovWuthrichZhu2021, CattaneoFengTitiunik2021: adjacent inference/sensitivity procedures, but no named scalar-only sharpness claim is targeted

novelty_flags.label: Conjecture 2
novelty_flags.one_line: The scalar-prefit impossibility does not name a published paper, estimator, or workflow that claims a uniformly sharp deterministic interval from only (q, eta, c); the cited papers are adjacent inference/sensitivity comparators rather than the refuted scalar rule.

strengthening_paths.suggestion: Either name a concrete scalar-prefit sensitivity practice from the literature for Conjecture 2, with the exact input restriction it uses, or demote Conjecture 2 to a corollary of Theorem 2 rather than a novelty claim.

State a clean sufficient condition for Conjecture 1's open class, for example full row rank of the augmented matrix plus an interior simplex solution and an explicit null direction whose exit distance gives delta.

Add one worked nondegenerate panel example with two prefit-equivalent weights and compute both radii, to separate the flagship from the archived row-span formula and make the field-tier contribution legible.

recommended_next_step: Revise Conjecture 2's target and add an explicit Conjecture 1 open-class witness/condition; the main kernel otherwise survives at the requested field tier.
